notes: CDN-available libraries for frontend concept generation. Agents pick 0-5 libraries per pass based on style fit. Each library has style-fit tags and a usage prompt to guide the agent. Libraries are OPTIONAL — pure CSS is always valid. The agent should choose libraries that genuinely enhance the design, not use them for the sake of using them.

animation.description: Core animation engines for timelines, morphing, spring physics, and complex choreography

libraries.name: GSAP

plugins.name: MorphSVG
plugins.note: Club plugin — use CSS clip-path morphing as free alternative

libraries.prompt: Use GSAP for complex multi-step animation timelines, scroll-driven choreography, and spring physics. Best when you need precise control over timing, staggering, and easing. Pair with ScrollTrigger for scroll-driven reveals.

libraries.name: Anime.js
libraries.prompt: Use Anime.js for lightweight staggered animations, SVG path drawing, and timeline sequences. Good alternative to GSAP when you need less weight. Excellent for staggered grid reveals and SVG line animations.

libraries.name: Motion One
libraries.prompt: Ultra-lightweight (~3kb) animation library with spring physics built in. Use when you want buttery smooth micro-interactions without the weight of GSAP. Great for hover states, toggle animations, and entrance effects.

scroll.description: Scroll behavior libraries for smooth scrolling, parallax, and scroll-triggered effects

libraries.name: Lenis
libraries.prompt: Premium smooth-scroll library that replaces native scrolling with butter-smooth inertia. Creates a luxury feel. Pair with GSAP ScrollTrigger for scroll-driven animations. Best for styles that feel heavy, premium, or fluid.

libraries.name: AOS (Animate On Scroll)
libraries.prompt: Zero-config scroll reveal library. Add data-aos attributes to HTML elements and they animate on scroll. Great for quick, consistent scroll reveals without writing JS animation code. Supports fade, slide, zoom, and flip presets.

libraries.name: ScrollReveal
libraries.prompt: Scroll-triggered reveal library with more control than AOS. Configure origin direction, distance, rotation, and scale per element. Good for cascading card reveals and staggered content entrances.

visual-effects.description: Particle systems, animated backgrounds, and visual effect libraries

libraries.name: tsParticles
libraries.prompt: Particle engine for ambient background effects. Use sparingly — particles should enhance atmosphere, not distract. Bubbles fit liquid, stars/dust fit slate, confetti fits retro-50s celebrations. Keep particle counts low (20-50) for performance.

libraries.name: Vanta.js
libraries.prompt: Animated 3D backgrounds using Three.js. Waves for liquid style, fog for slate, dots for mid-century. Use as a hero section background or full-page ambient layer. Performance-heavy — use in hero sections only, not full page. Requires Three.js as dependency.

libraries.name: Canvas Confetti
libraries.prompt: Lightweight confetti burst library. Use for celebration moments — idea submitted, project completed, milestone reached. Fire from specific button positions for targeted feedback. Very retro-50s energy.

libraries.name: Rough.js
libraries.prompt: Renders graphics with a hand-drawn, sketchy look using canvas. Use for whiteboard view, schema planner connections, or chart rendering. Creates an organic, imperfect aesthetic that contrasts with digital precision.

libraries.name: Vivus
libraries.prompt: SVG line-drawing animation library. Animates SVG paths as if being drawn by hand. Perfect for schema planner entity connections, whiteboard sketches, or decorative illustrations that draw themselves on view entrance.

interaction.description: Drag-and-drop, gestures, tooltips, and interactive element libraries

libraries.name: SortableJS
libraries.prompt: Drag-to-reorder library. Essential for kanban card reordering, directory tree rearrangement, and idea prioritization. Lightweight and framework-agnostic. Add drag handles for visual affordance.

libraries.name: Interact.js
libraries.prompt: Full interaction library for drag, resize, and multi-touch gestures. Use for whiteboard node dragging, panel resizing, and gesture-driven navigation. More powerful than SortableJS but heavier.

libraries.name: Tippy.js
libraries.prompt: Polished tooltip and popover library. Use for contextual information, abbreviation expansion, and action previews. Supports animation themes, interactive content, and positioning. Adds premium feel to any style.

libraries.name: Swiper
libraries.prompt: Touch-friendly carousel and slider library. Use for project card browsing, image galleries, or horizontal content scrolling. Supports parallax, creative effects, and pagination. Mobile-first design.

typography.description: Text animation and typographic effect libraries

libraries.name: Splitting.js
libraries.prompt: Splits text into individual characters, words, or lines for per-character CSS animation. Use for hero headlines that cascade in letter-by-letter, staggered word reveals, or glitch effects. Brutalist loves character-level disruption; liquid loves flowing cascades.

libraries.name: Typed.js
libraries.prompt: Typewriter text animation. Use for AI chat view to simulate typing responses, for hero section taglines, or for command-line style interfaces. Brutalist + terminal aesthetic is a natural fit.

lottie.description: Vector animation playback for complex pre-made animations

libraries.name: Lottie Web
libraries.prompt: Plays After Effects animations exported as JSON. Use for loading spinners, micro-interaction feedback, decorative illustrations, and onboarding animations. Download Lottie JSON files from LottieFiles.com and store in assets/ folder. Keep file sizes under 50kb each.
